items.text: Crossroads
Arrow Market
Enchantment Altar
More Arrows!
Rest Camp
You arrive at a crossroads lit by ancient runes.
A clear spring bubbles between the rocks, and a broken stele still hums with magic.
Inspect the Runes
Drink from the Spring
Offer an Arrow
A traveling merchant opens a case of arrows, their elements clashing inside.
He can deal with your extra arrows, or hand you a new one.
Remove an Arrow
Pick a New Arrow
The merchant slides over several elemental cores and waits for your choice.
The runes answer your arrow, and the patterns on the stele flare back to life.
You can keep guiding this power and change the arrows you draw later.
Guide the Magic
Take the Up Arrow
Take the Left Arrow
Take the Down Arrow
Take the Right Arrow
Brand with Kindling
Brand with Flow
Brand with Liquefy
You find an enchantment altar humming under its breath.
It can carve different spell marks into your arrows.
View Other Marks
Symbols on the altar back slowly flip over, revealing two more marks.
You review the spellwork from the last fight in the trial logs. Next round, you can draw arrows faster.
Draw Count +1
You light a small campfire under a sheltered cliff.
Warmth drives away fatigue, restoring some of your HP.
Leave
The spring washes away your travel fatigue. You heal 10 HP.
You toss an arrow into the rock crack. The runes flash briefly, then go still.
Magic flows into your deck. From now on, you draw 1 extra arrow each turn.
An Up arrow drops into your arrow sequence.
A Left arrow drops into your arrow sequence.
A Down arrow drops into your arrow sequence.
A Right arrow drops into your arrow sequence.
A Kindling mark appears on the arrow surface.
A Flow mark appears on the arrow surface.
The arrow edge glows, and it can now also count as a Down arrow.
Leave
Midnight Convenience Store
At 2 a.m., pink neon overexposes the store entrance like a dream.
Only three items remain on the shelves, and the barcode scanner is still muttering to itself.
Compressed Biscuits
Energy Drink
Mint Candy
The biscuit tastes like dust inside a server case, but the calories are real. You heal.
The drink flushes out a stray memory. Choose an arrow and remove it from your deck.
Mint bursts on your tongue, and a new item ad pops up.
You leave without checking out. The door sensor whispers, "See you next time."
Capsule Machine
An old capsule machine is wedged at the end of the alley, colored static floating inside its clear shell.
Its coin slot blinks. A sticker nearby says, "Win rate depends on mood."
Insert Coin
Kick It
Three capsules roll out, each containing a copy of the same arrow.
Plastic shells scatter everywhere. You get hurt, but you pry an arrow from the machine belly.
You stare at it for a while and decide not to make eye contact with RNG.
Anonymous Forum
You scroll through an anonymous forum. Every avatar is a default icon, but the posts refresh like living things.
A hot thread flickers: "You are here already. Say something."
Argue
Post
You win an argument with no audience, delete an annoying arrow, and recover some HP.
After posting, the comments bite back at your sanity, but you also earn an item upgrade.
You close the page. The background still pops up with "Someone replied to you."
Coffee or Cola?
The vending machine screen is split in two: coffee on one side, cola on the other.
It asks, "Awake, or even more awake?"
I Will Take Both
Coffee
Cola
You mix both cans and drink them. It tastes dangerous, but your HP and max HP both rise.
The bitterness pulls you back to reality. You heal.
Bubbles race through your veins. Your max HP increases.
You press refund. The machine spits out a cheap sigh.
Limited-Time Offer!
A mall billboard recognizes your face as a "potential impulse buyer."
Popups pile up: only now, miss it and wait forever.
Nice Clothes
Nice Weapon
Save 100% by Not Buying
New clothes fit like armor. Your max HP increases.
The weapon is only a sticker, but it really changes your item.
You successfully resist consumerism. The system gives you 2 Gold as a retention reward.
Referral Link
A popup chases you: "Share with friends and get a reward now!"
Fine print: this event may cause mild social death or HP loss.
Share Link
Share Link Again
The link is out and the Gold is in. The popup gets more excited: share again?
You close the popup. It still tries to persuade you during the closing animation.
Traffic Growth
A data curve suddenly spikes, like a neon snake biting into your turn.
Draw Count +1
You learn to grab arrows faster. From now on, draw count +1 each turn.
Go Home and Sleep
You cannot keep staying up tonight, so you decide to rest properly.
Kick It
Punch It
You punch the capsule machine. The coin slot coughs up two coins, and your hand starts aching.
Reply
You make a sincere reply. A stranger approval becomes brief security. At the start of your next battle, gain 4 Shield.
The coffee is as bitter as a to-do list. You wake up and decide to delete an arrow that should not stay.
Plain Water Is Healthier
You pour a cup of plain water. Your body does not complain and quietly recovers a little.
You close every popup and realize the best deal is buying nothing. The system reluctantly spits out 2 Gold as a "rational spending reward."
Know When to Stop
You post a clear video online. Within minutes, views surge like a neon tide. Comments and likes crowd the screen, and suddenly the next journey feels faster.
Heck Yeah!!!
The platform traffic pushes you forward. From now on, draw count +1 each turn.
Remove
Add
Arrow Recycling Station
An old terminal is set into the wall, its screen flashing "Recycle extra arrows."
It reads your played arrow sequence and starts a recycling process at matching strength.
Light Recycling
Deep Recycling
The terminal compresses an arrow into a hot data shard. Your deck feels a little lighter.
The terminal slams down twice, turning two arrows into tiny motes of light. Your deck feels much lighter.
You close the recycling program, and the terminal screen slowly goes dark.
Arrow Claw Machine
A claw machine glows with neon lights, its glass cabinet packed with Up, Down, Left, and Right arrows.
The claw hangs in midair and only responds to arrows of matching direction.
Grab Up Arrow
Grab Down Arrow
Grab Left Arrow
Grab Right Arrow
The claw wobbles down and picks up a glowing Up arrow. The new arrow drops into your deck.
The claw wobbles down and picks up a glowing Down arrow. The new arrow drops into your deck.
The claw wobbles down and picks up a glowing Left arrow. The new arrow drops into your deck.
The claw wobbles down and picks up a glowing Right arrow. The new arrow drops into your deck.
You release the joystick, and the arrows inside keep flashing neon.
Screen Protector Stall
A roadside stall displays rows of glowing screen protectors. The vendor says each one can stick to an arrow.
He flicks a scraper with practiced ease. Bubbles under the neon look like tiny item accidents.
Free Protector
Cheap Film
Tempered Glass
The free film fits surprisingly well. Choose an arrow and let it stay in your hand a little longer.
The film cracks into pretty patterns as soon as it sticks. Choose an arrow and make it fragile.
The tempered glass clicks sharply. Choose an arrow and make it return next turn.
Printer Accident
A self-service printer is spitting out direction arrows, steam rising from the paper edges.
The screen says "Print Successful," but you are sure nothing is aligned.
Cannot Tell
Wrong Size
Misaligned
You decide to pretend you do not understand. The printer refunds two coins as compensation for emotional damage.
The print ratio stretches. Choose an arrow and turn it into a larger multi-slot arrow.
The same arrow was printed twice. Choose an arrow and make it heavier when read.
Website Emergency
You open a website that should display a map, but the page starts shaking wildly.
Error messages stack up like monsters rushing to sign up.
Just Refresh
Page Not Responding
So Many Popups
You press refresh. The page briefly returns to normal, and your spirit recovers a little too.
The page freezes on a loading spinner. Choose an arrow and make it stay in hand at turn end.
The more popups you close, the more appear. Choose an arrow and teach it to copy itself after play.
Omni Night Sign
At 3 a.m., the wayfinding sign in an abandoned mall is still lit. Pink-purple arrows flicker through empty corridors—exit, restroom, arcade, underground parking... until all arrows point at you.
Follow All the Arrows
Take One Photo
The sign goes dark all at once, then lights up again in your hand. You gain an omni symbol.
The photo blurs into purple light, but someone is willing to pay for weird photos.
Suspected Trojan?
A constantly changing arrow flashes on your phone. Maybe you downloaded a Trojan? The phone is old enough that it finds no way to attack, so it just keeps blinking uselessly.
Fine As It Is
Reinstall System
You decide to leave it alone. The flashing arrow jumps into your deck, still changing direction.
The progress bar reaches 100%, and your wallet is cleared too. Every arrow looks freshly reset.
Missing Fonts
A night-shift print shop computer pops up a stream of garbled text. Every font is gone except a few stubborn symbols. After some clicks, a "Disable compatibility mode?" popup appears.
Disable Compatibility Mode
Keep Compatibility Mode
The screen goes black for a moment, then spits out a symbol before it can become text. You gain a random syntax symbol.
You leave things as they are. The print shop owner appreciates your restraint and slips you a few coins.
Signpost
You arrive at a three-way crossing. A sign in front of you reads, "Geniuses left, lunatics right."
Go Left
Go Right
Why Turn?
You choose left. Your max HP increases.
You choose right. The pain and tradeoff both feel real.
You refuse to turn, so you receive an item reward.
